blurb: Plans, builds, operates, and maintains Alberta's highway network, trade corridors, water infrastructure, and municipal transportation -- connecting communities and enabling economic growth.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: Alberta has a safe and efficient transportation system
goals.description: The transportation system is safe and secure, supported by policies, programs, educational opportunities, and enhanced services protecting public safety

supporting.name: Get a driver's licence (for a car)
supporting.desc: The full Class 5 journey: learner's permit (Class 7) → knowledge and road tests → GDL probationary licence → full Class 5 after 2-year probation period. New residents can exchange an out-of-province licence.
supporting.users: New drivers (16+), GDL holders upgrading to full licence, new residents exchanging out-of-province licences

legislation.full_name: Traffic Safety Act  -  s.51: Operator's Licences (Part 3, Div 1)

relationships.service: Provide driver training and testing services

supporting.name: Get a driver's licence (for a motorcycle)
supporting.desc: GDL steps: Class 6 learner → Class 6 GDL → full Class 6 after 2-year probation period
supporting.users: Motorcycle riders (16+, must hold at least a Class 7 learner)

legislation.full_name: Traffic Safety Act  -  s.51: Operator's Licences (Part 3, Div 1)

supporting.name: Get a commercial driver's licence
supporting.desc: Obtain a Class 1, 2, 3, or 4 licence through knowledge and road testing. Includes mandatory entry-level training (MELT) for Class 1 and Class 2 licences.

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: Alberta's economic corridors are expanded to enable market access
goals.description: Strategic economic corridor investments and innovation support Alberta's economic growth

foundational_services.name: Highway pavement resurfacing and repair
foundational_services.desc: Rehabilitation and surface treatment across 64,000+ lane km of provincial highways to maintain ride quality and safety

legislation.full_name: Highways Development and Protection Act

mandate_tags.tip: Maintain and rehabilitate highway infrastructure

foundational_services.name: Bridge inspection, construction, and rehabilitation
foundational_services.desc: Ongoing maintenance and lifecycle management of ~4,900 provincial bridges and interchanges

legislation.full_name: Highways Development and Protection Act

mandate_tags.tip: Maintain and rehabilitate highway infrastructure

foundational_services.name: Regional highway construction delivery
foundational_services.desc: Project delivery across North Central, Central, Peace, and South regions — managed by regional offices with contracted construction firms

legislation.full_name: Highways Development and Protection Act

mandate_tags.tip: Maintain and rehabilitate highway infrastructure

foundational_services.name: Highway twinning and capacity expansion
foundational_services.desc: Multi-year construction programs to increase capacity on critical trade routes (e.g., Highway 3 Taber–Lethbridge, Highway 686 northern corridor)

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: Alberta communities are supported with reliable infrastructure
goals.description: Alberta's transportation network and water management infrastructure is maintained effectively and responsibly, ensuring long-term sustainability and affordability

foundational_services.name: Flood mitigation infrastructure delivery
foundational_services.desc: $180M in water management and flood mitigation capital projects protecting Alberta communities from flood risk

mandate_tags.tip: Deliver flood mitigation infrastructure

main_services.name: Get provincial funding for municipal infrastructure

supporting.desc: Request STIP funding for local roads, bridges, and transit projects

supporting.desc: Browse the grants portal to identify eligible funding programs

supporting.desc: View provincial and federal grant allocations across Alberta

supporting.desc: Request Government of Alberta loans for eligible municipal capital projects

supporting.desc: Request grants for municipal water supply, treatment, disposal facilities, and regional water systems through the Water for Life program
